methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Chef Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Rajat Bhageria that has raised $65.6M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—including peers such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

A peer-median approach is not applicable here due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) required to generate three or more datapoints. A stage-progression method is therefore the primary lens. Using total capital raised of $66M as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $131M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $112M (low) to $151M (high), centered on a $131M median. This range reflects the single applicable methodology and carries material uncertainty given the absence of granular peer comparables at Chef Robotics' specific stage and tier. Confidence in the range is constrained by reliance on a stage-progression heuristic without cross-validation via market multiples. Additional comparables or disclosure of Chef Robotics' revenue and capital efficiency metrics would materially tighten this band.
